title: cornus rugosa
intro: Cornus rugosa, also known as the roundleaf dogwood, is a shrub native to North America, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The bark and roots of the Cornus rugosa plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating diarrhea, and acting as an antibacterial agent. Cornus rugosa can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to leverage its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
